what_it_does: Implements shared-element transitions (FlipBridge) using the FLIP technique and a frozen outgoing-screen overlay (ScreenGhost) so that elements marked with data-flip appear to travel continuously between screens while non-shared pieces fly off in parallel.
observations: FlipBridge.capture records viewport rectangles of all [data-flip] elements into a key map.
FlipBridge.run measures destination rectangles, suppresses entrance animations with a temporary flip-freeze class, and creates fixed-position clones in a body-level .flip-layer.
Clones are transformed from the previous rectangle to the current one, then removed; the real element is hidden during travel and revealed afterward.
Animation is skipped entirely when the document is hidden to avoid stranded clones in background tabs.

ScreenGhost.spawn clones the current .welcome/.wizard/.studio-root screen, makes its background transparent, hides [data-flip] elements and .wall elements, and applies an exit class so the old screen can animate out over the incoming one.
Durations and easing are read from CSS custom properties --scr-ease, --scr-exit-dur, and --scr-flip-dur with fallbacks.
standouts: Uses a top-layer clone for the traveling element rather than moving the real DOM node, avoiding stacking-context and ancestor-animation problems.
Pairs the FLIP bridge with a transparent exit ghost so old and new screens animate simultaneously.
Freezes entrance/exit animations during measurement to get accurate destination rectangles.
conventions: Vanilla JS IIFE exposing window.FlipBridge and window.ScreenGhost.
data-* attribute keyed matching between outgoing and incoming elements.
getBoundingClientRect for geometry; requestAnimationFrame double-call to commit styles before starting transition.
transitionend listener plus a fallback setTimeout for cleanup.
CSS custom properties for timing and easing configuration.
concerns: cloneNode(true) copies DOM but not JavaScript state, event listeners, or form values, so interactive shared elements may behave incorrectly during the transition.
Inline style.animation = 'none' on the real element persists after cleanup and may unintentionally suppress future CSS animations until styles are reset.
If the transitionend event never fires, the fallback timeout eventually cleans up, but the clone could remain interactive/focusable until then.
The .flip-layer div is created once and never removed even when empty.
ScreenGhost removes [data-flip] attributes from the cloned snapshot but leaves other interactive elements that may still receive pointer events unless CSS disables them.
No handling for elements that change size or scroll during the transition after measurement.
notes: A self-contained FLIP transition helper that trades moving the real element for a top-layer clone and overlays a transparent exit snapshot to keep old-screen and shared-element motion in sync.
questions: What code calls FlipBridge.capture and FlipBridge.run, and how is it synchronized with route/navigation changes?
Where are the CSS rules for .flip-layer, .flip-freeze, .screen-ghost, welcome--exit, wizard--exit, and studio--exit defined?
How are the CSS custom properties --scr-ease, --scr-exit-dur, and --scr-flip-dur set and themed?
How are interactive shared elements (e.g., text inputs, focused buttons) kept coherent when their visible clone is not the real element?
What determines whether the current root is .welcome, .wizard, or .studio-root?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it captures shared-element geometry, animates them via clones, and overlays an exit ghost for synchronized transitions.
apparent_themes: Continuous surface / spatial continuity across navigation.
Layering and occlusion management with body-level overlays.
Graceful degradation (skip animation when document is hidden).
intention_signals: The code is trying to make screen changes feel like a single interface surface rearranging itself, where persistent elements physically travel to their new locations while the rest of the page exits and enters around them.
novelty: Distinctive combination of a top-layer FLIP clone with a transparent, aria-hidden exit ghost so non-shared and shared animations can run concurrently without one waiting for the other.
contribution: Provides a reusable, self-contained mechanism for keyed shared-element transitions and a synchronized outgoing-screen snapshot, capturing geometry, animating clones, and revealing real elements after travel.
summary_for_embedding: flip.js implements a vanilla-JS FLIP shared-element transition bridge and a screen-ghost overlay that captures data-flip element rectangles, animates fixed-position clones in a body-level layer, and freezes an outgoing screen snapshot so old and new screens can transition together.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make navigation between .welcome, .wizard, and .studio-root screens feel like one continuous surface by moving shared elements while the rest of the screen flies off and in.

symbols.does: Records viewport rectangles of all visible [data-flip] elements keyed by their data-flip value.
symbols.description: Iterates document.querySelectorAll('[data-flip]'), stores left/top/width/height for each element with non-zero size, and returns a map used as the 'from' state for FLIP animations.

symbols.does: Creates or returns a fixed body-level div (.flip-layer) that sits above the screen ghost.
symbols.description: Ensures a single top-level container exists for traveling clones so they are not occluded by the outgoing screen snapshot or lower stacking contexts.

symbols.does: Measures destination rectangles and animates clones from their previous position/size to the current one.
symbols.description: Accepts a fromMap and options, freezes entrance animations, computes per-element transform deltas, creates fixed clones in the top layer, hides the real elements, plays transform transitions, and reveals the real elements after the clone lands.

symbols.does: Removes the traveling clone and reveals the real element after the transition.
symbols.description: Defined inside run for each animated element; removes the clone, clears its transitionend listener, restores realEl visibility, and sets its animation to none to prevent a replay.

symbols.does: Transitionend handler that triggers cleanup only for transform events on the clone.
symbols.description: Defined inside run; filters transitionend events by target and propertyName so unrelated transitions do not prematurely end the travel animation.
